torch_pattern: out = torch.matmul(input, weight); out = out + bias if bias is not None else out; out = fused_activation(out) if fused_activation is not None else out

config_template.config: get_matmul_config({M}, {K}, {N}, {CORE_GRID}, {DEFAULT_BLOCK_SIZE})  # MinimalMatmulConfig with in0_block_w, per_core_M/N, subblock_h={SB_H}, subblock_w={SB_W}, compute_with_storage_grid_size={CORE_GRID}
config_template.compute_kernel_config: {COMPUTE_KERNEL_CONFIG}  # e.g. hifi2 / hifi2_fp16 / lofi depending on precision regime
config_template.fused_activation: {ACT_FN}  # optional unary activation fused into the matmul epilogue

weight_transform: weight is pre-transposed to [K, N] layout (ttnn linear convention, weight_tensor.padded_shape[-1] == N); bias broadcast over M; weights converted to device tiles ahead of time (e.g. self.w2, self.wqkv, self.proj_weight.data)
applicability_notes: torch_pattern_source: call_site (alternation branch with ttnn.linear: minimal_matmul(input_tensor=x, weight_tensor=w, bias_tensor=b, fused_activation=act) is a drop-in replacement for ttnn.linear, i.e. activation(x @ W + b)); fusion_notes: Fuses linear (matmul + bias-add) + optional unary activation into a single op. Used in model code as the prefill-path replacement for ttnn.linear when seq_len > 128 (and mode != DECODE). Inputs with seq_len > MAX_QKV_MM_SEQ_LEN are pre-reshaped to [1, seq_len // MAX, MAX, -1] before the matmul and reshaped back after. Some call sites note it is NOT always a win (commented out where 'not giving any performance improvement over linear' or 'giving bad outputs for seqlen > 128').

placement_observations.size_descriptor: prefill activations, seq_len > 128 (w1/w3 FF matmuls, M = seq_len, N = hidden/ff_dim)
placement_observations.source: call_site: w1_out/w3_out = ttnn.experimental.minimal_matmul(..., memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG) in else-branch of seq_len gate

placement_observations.size_descriptor: attention output projection (WO) prefill, M = seq_len
QKV fused prefill input x_11SH; reshaped to [1, seq_len // MAX_QKV_MM_SEQ_LEN, MAX_QKV_MM_SEQ_LEN, -1] when seq_len exceeds MAX_QKV_MM_SEQ_LEN
placement_observations.source: call_site: xqkv_fused = ttnn.experimental.minimal_matmul(x_11SH, self.wqkv, ...) selected when seq_len > 128, else ttnn.linear

placement_observations.size_descriptor: FF2 (w2) prefill output, seq_len > 128 and mode != DECODE
placement_observations.source: call_site: w2_out = ttnn.experimental.minimal_matmul(w2_in, self.w2, compute_kernel_config=li_ff2_compute_kernel_cfg, config=pc_2) in seq_len>128 branch, ttnn.linear otherwise

placement_observations.size_descriptor: generic linear layer output (M, K, N read from x.padded_shape and weight.padded_shape)
placement_observations.source: call_site: generic Linear.forward wrapper computes per-shape MinimalMatmulConfig from M,K,N and core grid

placement_observations.size_descriptor: prefill matmul output (alternate model), dtype bfloat8_b
placement_observations.source: call_site (commented-out variant): ttnn.experimental.minimal_matmul(..., dtype=ttnn.bfloat8_b, memory_config=ttnn.DRAM_MEMORY_CONFIG) — noted as no perf improvement over ttnn.linear at this site
